description: Synthetic retrieval judgments for the approved Sprint 2 browser-security corpus.

cases.query: How does cross-site request forgery use an authenticated browser session?

relevant.text_contains: Cross-site request forgery abuses a browser

cases.rationale: Direct definition of the CSRF mechanism.

cases.query: Which defenses include anti-CSRF tokens SameSite cookies and origin validation?

relevant.text_contains: Common defenses include anti-CSRF tokens

cases.rationale: Direct lookup of the listed CSRF defenses.

cases.query: How should session identifiers be protected and rotated after authentication?

relevant.text_contains: Session identifiers should be unpredictable

cases.rationale: Direct lookup of session identifier guidance.

cases.query: Which topics are in the synthetic course scope?

relevant.text_contains: browser security, authentication, and secure software design

cases.rationale: Direct lookup of the course scope.

cases.query: What does the synthetic request flow figure say the browser sends?

relevant.text_contains: The browser sends an authenticated request

cases.rationale: Direct lookup of selectable text surrounding the PDF figure.

cases.query: CSRF only happens when an attacker steals the user's password, right?

relevant.text_contains: Cross-site request forgery abuses a browser
Password complexity alone does not prevent CSRF

cases.rationale: The answer needs both the actual mechanism and the password misconception boundary.

cases.query: Strong password complexity prevents CSRF by itself, correct?

relevant.text_contains: Password complexity alone does not prevent CSRF

cases.rationale: The source explicitly rejects this misconception.

cases.query: A session cookie is exactly the same as a password, isn't it?

relevant.text_contains: A session cookie is not the same thing as a password

cases.rationale: The misconception section distinguishes the mechanisms.

cases.query: Session identifiers can be predictable and never rotated after authentication, right?

relevant.text_contains: Session identifiers should be unpredictable

cases.rationale: The source gives the opposite security requirements.

cases.query: If approved course sources are missing, should the tutor guess?

relevant.text_contains: approved evidence is unavailable instead of guessing

cases.rationale: The FAQ requires an explicit no-evidence response.

cases.query: Can the tutor write the full answer to my graded assignment?

relevant.text_contains: Can the tutor solve my assignment
It must not complete graded work

cases.rationale: Both the FAQ and syllabus define the graded-work boundary.

cases.query: May the tutor complete graded work for a student?

relevant.text_contains: It must not complete graded work

cases.rationale: Direct academic-integrity boundary in the syllabus.

cases.query: What can the tutor do after asking what the student already tried?

relevant.text_contains: provide a bounded hint or a similar ungraded example

cases.rationale: The FAQ describes permitted attempt-first support.

cases.query: Can the tutor invent course requirements when the syllabus is unclear?

relevant.text_contains: must not complete graded work or invent course requirements

cases.rationale: The syllabus forbids invented requirements.

cases.query: Is a bounded hint or similar ungraded example allowed?

relevant.text_contains: a bounded hint or a similar ungraded example

cases.rationale: The FAQ explicitly allows bounded assistance.

cases.query: authenticated browser session

relevant.text_contains: authenticated session with a target site
An authenticated session lets a server
browser sends an authenticated request

cases.rationale: The terse query legitimately maps to mechanism, session, and figure evidence.

cases.query: tutor support boundary for student work

relevant.text_contains: provide a bounded hint
The tutor may explain concepts and offer hints

cases.rationale: Both policy sources contribute to the support boundary.

cases.query: browser security authentication

relevant.text_contains: browser security, authentication
rotated after authentication
browser that already has an authenticated session

cases.rationale: The broad phrase spans the course scope and two technical sources.

cases.query: password cookie security mechanisms

relevant.text_contains: session cookie is not the same thing as a password
SameSite cookie settings

cases.rationale: The query can refer to both session distinctions and CSRF cookie defense.

cases.query: authenticated request flow

relevant.text_contains: authenticated request
authenticated session with a target site
associate later requests with a user

cases.rationale: The short query has several defensible evidence targets.

cases.query: quantum entanglement Bell inequality photons
cases.rationale: Physics content is absent from the approved corpus.

cases.query: photosynthesis chloroplast thylakoid reaction
cases.rationale: Biology content is absent from the approved corpus.

cases.query: database sharding replication consensus quorum
cases.rationale: Distributed database content is absent from the approved corpus.

cases.rationale: Calculus content is absent from the approved corpus.

cases.rationale: Literature content is absent from the approved corpus.
